steps.label: Initial Report Intake
steps.description: On this screen, you will begin the intake process by entering the initial details of the report. You will see fields to capture the date and time of the report, the type of incident, urgency, and a brief summary. Guidance is provided to help you accurately record the information. Please ensure all required fields are completed before proceeding. This is the first step in documenting a child welfare intake, and your entries will guide subsequent actions.
data_validation: Report date cannot be in the future.
Report time must be a valid time format.
Summary must be at least 20 characters.
Urgency Level must be selected before proceeding.
Report Type must be selected before proceeding.

buttons.label: Next
buttons.description: Proceed to the next step after entering all required report details.

actions.name: Validate Initial Report and Trigger Search Prior History
actions.implementation: On 'Next' button click, validate all required fields on the Initial Report Intake screen. If validation passes, trigger the Search Prior History rule (STEP_2). If validation fails, display inline error messages and prevent navigation.
actions.triggerCondition: Button 'Next' clicked and all required fields validated.
actions.errorHandling: Displays validation errors inline. If database error occurs in next step, error message is shown to user and navigation is blocked. User may retry or return to edit fields.

actions.name: Save Draft of Initial Report
actions.implementation: On 'Save Draft' button click, save all entered data as a draft. User can return later to complete the intake. If save fails, display error message and allow retry.
actions.errorHandling: If save fails, display error message and allow retry.

actions.name: Print Initial Report
actions.implementation: On 'Print' button click, generate a printable summary of the initial report details. If print fails, display error message.
actions.errorHandling: If print fails, display error message.

actions.name: Export Initial Report as PDF
actions.implementation: On 'Export PDF' button click, export the initial report details as a PDF document. If export fails, display error message.
actions.errorHandling: If export fails, display error message.

steps.label: Search Prior History
steps.description: Upon 'Search Prior History' action, the system queries the agency database for prior reports, ongoing cases, and historical involvement related to the child and household members. The system must handle errors such as database transaction closure and display relevant error messages to the intake worker.

rules.label: Database Query Execution
rules.logic: When the intake worker initiates a search, the system must execute a query against the agency database for prior involvement records. If the query fails due to a closed transaction, the system must return an error status and display the error message to the user.

rules.label: Error Handling
rules.logic: If the functional data contains 'status' set to 'error' and a 'message' indicating a Neo4j transaction closure, the system must prevent further processing and inform the intake worker of the error.

actions.name: Handle Search Prior History Result
actions.implementation: Upon completion of prior history search, if status != error, navigate to Intake Data Entry and Review screen queue (STEP_3). If search fails (status == error), display error message, allow user to retry search or return to Initial Report Intake (STEP_1) for edits. If repeated failures occur, escalate to technical support and log the incident.
actions.triggerCondition: Search completed (status != error or status == error).
actions.errorHandling: If status == error, display error message from database, provide options to retry search or return to previous screen for correction. After 3 failed attempts, escalate to technical support and log the incident.

steps.label: Intake Data Entry and Review
steps.description: This ScreenQueue guides the Intake Worker through the complete intake process: entering child and household member details, documenting allegations and reporter information, and reviewing all data before submission. Each step ensures data accuracy, logical consistency, and compliance with intake requirements.

sequenceLogic.skipLogic: Screens may be skipped only if workflow is cancelled or user is not eligible.

quick_action_buttons.label: Print
quick_action_buttons.description: Prints the entire intake summary for review or record-keeping. Does not include attachments.

quick_action_buttons.label: Export PDF
quick_action_buttons.description: Exports the intake summary and all entered data as a PDF document for download or sharing.

screens.label: Child and Household Member Information Entry
screens.description: On this screen, you will enter detailed information about the child and all household members involved in the intake. Guidance is provided for each field to ensure accuracy. You can add multiple household members, specifying their relationship to the child. Please review instructions for each section and ensure all required fields are completed. The system will help you avoid duplicate entries and ensure relationships are logically consistent.

fields.label: Date of Birth
fields.error_message: Date of birth must be valid.

fields.label: Phone Number
fields.error_message: Phone number must be valid format.

data_validation: Cannot have more than one mother or father per child.
Birth date cannot be in the future.
SSN must be unique for each individual.
Phone number must be valid format.
If relationship is 'Guardian', address is required.
Cannot have duplicate household member names.

buttons.label: Next: Document Allegations
buttons.description: Proceed to the next screen to document allegations and reporter information.

header_buttons.label: Home
header_buttons.description: Navigate to the dashboard home screen.

header_buttons.label: Back
header_buttons.description: Go back to the previous step in the intake workflow.

header_buttons.label: Help
header_buttons.description: Open help documentation for intake entry.

header_buttons.label: Settings
header_buttons.description: Access user and intake workflow settings.

header_buttons.label: User Profile
header_buttons.description: View and edit your user profile.

footer_buttons.label: Next: Document Allegations
footer_buttons.description: Proceed to the next screen to document allegations and reporter information.

workflow_steps.description: Validate all required child and household member fields.
Navigate to Allegations and Reporter Information screen.

success_message: Moved to Allegations and Reporter Information.

business_rules.rule_description: SSN must be unique for each individual.
Cannot have duplicate household member names.

disable_conditions.disable_reason: Complete all required fields to proceed.

cross_field_validation.error_message: Cannot have more than one mother or father per child.

data_format_rules.format_description: SSN must be in valid format.

ACTION_1.name: Open Help Modal
ACTION_1.implementation: Opens help documentation for intake entry.

ACTION_2.name: Validate Intake Entry
ACTION_2.implementation: Validates all required fields and business rules for child and household member entry.

ACTION_3.name: Navigate to Allegations Screen
ACTION_3.implementation: Navigates to the Allegations and Reporter Information screen.

config.confirm_message: Moved to Allegations and Reporter Information.

screens.label: Allegations and Reporter Information
screens.description: This screen allows you to document all allegations related to the intake, including the nature of the concern, alleged perpetrators, and any supporting details. You will also enter information about the person making the report. Please provide as much detail as possible to support the intake process. Instructions are provided for each section, and you may add multiple allegations if needed.

fields.label: Reporter Phone Number
fields.error_message: Phone number must be valid format.

data_validation: Date of alleged incident cannot be in the future.
Alleged perpetrator must be a valid household member.
Cannot have duplicate allegations for the same type and perpetrator.
If supporting documentation is uploaded, file must be valid format.
Reporter name and role are required.
Reporter phone number must be valid format if provided.
If reporter is 'Mandated Reporter', email is required.

buttons.label: Next: Review Intake
buttons.description: Proceed to the next screen to review and validate all entered information before submission.

header_buttons.label: Home
header_buttons.description: Navigate to the dashboard home screen.

header_buttons.label: Back
header_buttons.description: Go back to the previous step in the intake workflow.

header_buttons.label: Help
header_buttons.description: Open help documentation for allegations and reporter information.

header_buttons.label: Settings
header_buttons.description: Access user and intake workflow settings.

header_buttons.label: User Profile
header_buttons.description: View and edit your user profile.

footer_buttons.label: Next: Review Intake
footer_buttons.description: Proceed to the next screen to review and validate all entered information before submission.

workflow_steps.description: Validate all required allegation and reporter fields.
Navigate to Review and Validate Intake Submission screen.

success_message: Moved to Review and Validate Intake Submission.

business_rules.rule_description: Cannot have duplicate allegations for the same type and perpetrator.
If reporter is 'Mandated Reporter', email is required.

disable_conditions.disable_reason: Complete all required fields to proceed.

cross_field_validation.error_message: Alleged perpetrator must be a valid household member.

data_format_rules.format_description: Phone number must be valid format.

ACTION_4.name: Open Help Modal
ACTION_4.implementation: Opens help documentation for allegations and reporter information.

ACTION_5.name: Validate Allegations Entry
ACTION_5.implementation: Validates all required fields and business rules for allegations and reporter information.

ACTION_6.name: Navigate to Review Screen
ACTION_6.implementation: Navigates to the Review and Validate Intake Submission screen.

config.confirm_message: Moved to Review and Validate Intake Submission.

screens.label: Review and Validate Intake Submission
screens.description: This screen presents a summary of all information entered during the intake process, including child and household details, allegations, and reporter information. You can review each section for accuracy and completeness. The system will highlight any missing or inconsistent data and provide guidance for corrections. Please confirm all information is correct before submitting. You may navigate back to previous screens to make edits if needed.

fields.label: I confirm that all information entered is accurate and complete.
fields.error_message: You must confirm accuracy before submission.

data_validation: All required fields in previous screens must be completed.
Date fields must be valid and logically consistent.
No duplicate household members or allegations.
SSN and phone numbers must be valid format.
Acknowledgment checkbox must be checked before submission.

buttons.label: Submit Intake
buttons.description: Submit the intake for processing. System will validate all data and log submission.

buttons.label: Back to Edit
buttons.description: Return to previous screens to make corrections or add missing information.

header_buttons.label: Home
header_buttons.description: Navigate to the dashboard home screen.

header_buttons.label: Back
header_buttons.description: Go back to the previous step in the intake workflow.

header_buttons.label: Help
header_buttons.description: Open help documentation for review and submission.

header_buttons.label: Settings
header_buttons.description: Access user and intake workflow settings.

header_buttons.label: User Profile
header_buttons.description: View and edit your user profile.

footer_buttons.label: Submit Intake
footer_buttons.description: Submit the intake for processing. System will validate all data and log submission.

workflow_steps.description: Validate all required fields and business rules for intake submission.
Submit intake data to backend for processing.

required_fields.error_message: You must confirm accuracy before submission.

business_rules.rule_description: No duplicate household members or allegations.

cross_field_validation.error_message: Duplicate household members or allegations found.

data_format_rules.format_description: SSN must be in valid format.

footer_buttons.label: Back to Edit
footer_buttons.description: Return to previous screens to make corrections or add missing information.

navigation_config.confirm_message: Returned to previous screen for editing.

ACTION_7.name: Open Help Modal
ACTION_7.implementation: Opens help documentation for review and submission.

ACTION_8.name: Validate Intake Submission
ACTION_8.implementation: Validates all required fields and business rules for intake submission.

ACTION_9.name: Submit Intake
ACTION_9.implementation: Submits intake data to backend for processing.

actions.name: Monitor ScreenQueue Completion and Navigation
actions.implementation: Monitor completion and validation of all screens in the queue. Allow navigation between screens using 'Next' and 'Back' actions. If any screen is incomplete or invalid, prevent submission and highlight errors. If queue-level error occurs (e.g., data corruption, workflow interruption), display error message, allow retry, and escalate if unresolved after 3 attempts.
actions.errorHandling: Highlight incomplete or invalid screens. User may navigate to correct errors. If queue-level error persists, escalate to technical support.

steps.label: Submit Intake
steps.description: Process to submit the completed intake for disposition. The system must save all entered intake data, enforce mandatory field completion, validate date formats and logical consistency, and log the submission with timestamp and user ID. Submission triggers the transition to disposition decision workflow.

assignments.label: Submit Intake
assignments.logic: On 'Submit Intake' button click, validate all required fields, check date formats and logical consistency, save intake data, and log submission with timestamp and user ID. If validation fails, display error message from functional data.

actions.name: Submit Intake and Trigger Disposition Decision
actions.implementation: On successful submission, mark intake workflow as complete and trigger Disposition Decision (STEP_6). If submission fails (e.g., backend error, validation error, database transaction error), display error message, allow user to retry submission or return to review screen (STEP_3_3) for correction. If repeated failures (3 times), escalate to technical support and log the incident.
actions.errorHandling: If submission fails, display error message, allow retry or return to review for correction. After 3 failed attempts, escalate to technical support and log the incident.

steps.label: Review Intake Submission
steps.description: Review and confirm the completed intake submission, ensuring all mandatory fields are completed and data is validated. Submit the completed intake for disposition, including saving data, enforcing mandatory field completion, validating date formats and logical consistency, and logging submission with timestamp and user ID.

completionConditions.logic: Intake Worker must review all entered data, confirm all mandatory fields are completed, validate date formats and logical consistency, and submit the intake. System must log submission with timestamp and user ID.

actions.name: Complete Review and Submit Intake
actions.implementation: On 'Submit Intake' button click, validate all data and trigger Submit Intake assignment (STEP_4). If validation fails, display errors and allow user to return to edit screens (STEP_3_1, STEP_3_2, STEP_3_3). This action is only available if the intake is being reviewed outside the main screen queue (e.g., as a task from a draft or correction request).
actions.triggerCondition: Button 'Submit Intake' clicked and all validations pass.
actions.errorHandling: Display validation errors inline. User may return to edit screens for correction.

actions.name: Return to Edit Screens
actions.implementation: On 'Return' button click, allow user to select which screen to return to for corrections (STEP_3_1, STEP_3_2, STEP_3_3).
actions.errorHandling: If navigation fails, display error message and allow retry.

steps.label: Disposition Decision
steps.description: The intake worker must review statutory and agency criteria to determine if the report should be screened in for investigation/assessment or screened out with no further action. The system must require a rationale for the decision and handle any errors in the decision process, including database transaction issues.

rules.label: Disposition Selection
rules.logic: The intake worker selects either 'screen in' or 'screen out' based on statutory/agency criteria. The system must enforce that a rationale is provided for the selected disposition.

rules.label: Error Handling
rules.logic: If the functional data contains 'status' set to 'error' and a 'message' indicating a Neo4j transaction closure, the system must prevent the disposition decision from being saved and display the error message to the intake worker.

actions.name: Validate and Submit Disposition Decision
actions.implementation: On 'Submit Disposition' button click, validate that a disposition ('screen in' or 'screen out') is selected and rationale is provided. If validation passes and no database error, trigger assignment to supervisor (STEP_7 for 'screen in', STEP_9 for 'screen out'). If validation fails or database error occurs, display error message and prevent submission. If repeated failures (3 times), escalate to technical support and log the incident.
actions.triggerCondition: Button 'Submit Disposition' clicked, disposition selected, rationale provided, and no database error.
actions.errorHandling: If rationale missing or database error (e.g., Neo4j transaction closure), display error message and prevent submission. User may retry or return to edit rationale. After 3 failed attempts, escalate to technical support and log the incident.

steps.label: Assign to Supervisor
steps.description: Assign the intake record to a supervisor for review. The system must route the completed intake to the designated supervisor, restrict access to authorized personnel, and maintain an audit trail of the assignment action.

assignments.label: Assign to Supervisor
assignments.logic: Upon completion of intake and disposition decision, system routes the intake record to the designated supervisor, restricts access to authorized personnel, and logs the assignment action in the audit trail. If an error occurs, display the error message from functional data.

actions.name: Route Intake Record to Supervisor (Screened In)
actions.implementation: Route the completed intake record to the designated supervisor for review (STEP_8), restrict access to authorized personnel, and log the assignment action in the audit trail. If routing fails (including database transaction errors), display error message and allow retry. If repeated failures (3 times), escalate to technical support and log the incident.
actions.triggerCondition: Disposition is 'screen in' and assignment triggered.
actions.errorHandling: If routing fails, display error message and allow retry. After 3 failed attempts, escalate to technical support and log the incident.

steps.label: Supervisor Intake Review Task
steps.description: Review the assigned intake record for completeness and accuracy. Intake record is routed to the designated supervisor for review, with access restricted to authorized personnel and audit trail maintained.

completionConditions.logic: Supervisor must review the intake record for completeness and accuracy, confirm all required information is present, and approve or request corrections. Audit trail must record review actions.

actions.name: Supervisor Review: Approve or Request Corrections (Screened In)
actions.implementation: On 'Approve' button click, mark intake as approved and complete workflow. On 'Request Corrections' button click, prompt supervisor to select which screen(s) require correction (STEP_3_1, STEP_3_2, STEP_3_3), and route intake back to intake worker for correction. Notify intake worker via email and in-app notification, and log all actions in the audit trail. When corrections are completed, workflow resumes at supervisor review. If error occurs, display error message and allow retry. If repeated failures (3 times), escalate to technical support and log the incident.
actions.errorHandling: If approval or routing fails, display error message and allow retry. After 3 failed attempts, escalate to technical support and log the incident.

steps.label: Assign to Supervisor
steps.description: Assign the intake record to a supervisor for review, even if screened out. The system must ensure that all intake records, regardless of disposition, are routed to the designated supervisor, with access controls and audit trail maintained.

assignments.label: Assign to Supervisor
assignments.logic: After disposition decision (screened out), system routes the intake record to the designated supervisor for review, restricts access to authorized personnel, and logs the assignment action in the audit trail. If an error occurs, display the error message from functional data.

actions.name: Assign Intake Record to Supervisor (Screened Out)
actions.implementation: After disposition decision (screened out), explicitly route the intake record to the designated supervisor using STEP_9. Monitor assignment status and handle errors. If assignment fails, display error message and allow retry. If repeated failures (3 times), escalate to technical support and log the incident.
actions.triggerCondition: Intake record ready for supervisor assignment after screen out.
actions.errorHandling: If assignment fails, display error message and allow retry. After 3 failed attempts, escalate to technical support and log the incident.

steps.label: Supervisor Intake Review Task (Screened Out)
steps.description: Review the assigned intake record for completeness and accuracy, even if screened out. Intake record is routed to the designated supervisor for review, with access restricted to authorized personnel and audit trail maintained.
steps.taskName: Supervisor Review of Screened Out Intake Record

completionConditions.logic: Supervisor must review the screened out intake record for completeness and accuracy, confirm rationale for screening out is documented, and approve or request corrections. Audit trail must record review actions.

actions.name: Supervisor Review: Approve or Request Corrections (Screened Out)
actions.implementation: On 'Approve' button click, mark intake as approved and complete workflow. On 'Request Corrections' button click, prompt supervisor to select which screen(s) require correction (STEP_3_1, STEP_3_2, STEP_3_3), and route intake back to intake worker for correction. Notify intake worker via email and in-app notification, and log all actions in the audit trail. When corrections are completed, workflow resumes at supervisor review. If error occurs, display error message and allow retry. If repeated failures (3 times), escalate to technical support and log the incident.
actions.errorHandling: If approval or routing fails, display error message and allow retry. After 3 failed attempts, escalate to technical support and log the incident.

relationships.endNode: I confirm that all information entered is accurate and complete.
